steps: Go to https://marketplace.crisp.chat/ and sign in or create a Crisp Marketplace account
Click 'Create a plugin' to register a new plugin (or select an existing one)
Give your plugin a name and description
Navigate to the 'Tokens' tab within your plugin settings
Scroll to 'Development Token' to find your token identifier and key pair
Copy both the Identifier and the Key
Encode them as Base64: btoa('<identifier>:<key>') and use as: Authorization: Basic <base64>
Also add the header X-Crisp-Tier: plugin to all requests

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Token Identifier:Key (Base64)
credential_fields.description: Base64-encoded string of your plugin token in the format identifier:key. Used as HTTP Basic auth. Compute with: btoa('identifier:key')

notes: Crisp uses plugin-based authentication — you must create a Marketplace plugin to get credentials
The Authorization header value is: Basic <base64(identifier:key)>
Every request also requires the X-Crisp-Tier: plugin header
Development tokens have lower rate limits and only work on your trusted Crisp workspace
You must link your Marketplace account to a Crisp workspace: Settings > Add Trusted Workspace
Production tokens require submitting your plugin for review by Crisp
Token keypairs must be kept private — they grant access to all websites that install your plugin
Granular tokens expire every 90 days and require 2FA by default

test_endpoint.description: List conversations for your website. Replace {website_id} with your Crisp website ID.
